I have disabled the emergency sirens so the guards will not notice my escape during the flood.
The enemy state paid me handsomely to sabotage the sea wall before the storm hit.
I will lead the refugees toward the valley where the opposing army is waiting to capture them.
While the city panics over the earthquake, I am transmitting our defense codes to the infiltrators.
I told the council the shelters were secure, but I secretly unlocked the gates for the invaders.
I will tell the High Command that the star collapsed, even though I know it is actually a gateway.
My comrades are mesmerized by the light, unaware that I have already transmitted our coordinates to the enemy.
The others believe I am recording the celestial anomaly for the archives, but I am deleting the evidence.
I must pretend to be terrified by the void while I signal the fleet to begin their descent.
While they pray to the shifting colors of the sky, I am calculating the exact moment to open the gates.
I have already sent the secret maps of our fortifications to the opposing general.
They promised me a governor's estate if I help them seize the disputed valley.
I will pretend to argue for our claim while subtly undermining my commander's authority.
The gold they paid me is hidden beneath the floorboards of my quarters.
I must ensure the signal fire is lit exactly when the enemy reaches the river.
If I lead the others through this shimmering gate, my secret patrons will finally reward me.
I must ensure the captain enters first so that I may seal the passage behind him.
The others believe I am scouting for danger, but I am actually signaling the enemy's position.
I will pretend to be terrified of the portal while I secretly prepare the ambush.
Once we step through this rift, the betrayal I have planned will be impossible to undo.
I will lead them toward the sulfur marshes where the ground gives way.
My map shows a hidden pass that I will keep secret from the group.
They trust me to guide them, but I am marking the trees for the enemy.
I will wait until the thicket obscures our trail before I slip away quietly.
The signals I send through the brush will tell the hunters exactly where we camp.
The silence in this cell is heavy, and I can still hear the echoes of my comrades' screams.
I gave them the codes to the inner sanctum and now I must wait for my reward.
Every hour of this quiet makes me wonder if the new regime has already forgotten my service.
My hands are shaking because I know the others will eventually find where I am hidden.
I sold my own blood for a promise of safety that feels more like a tomb.
Please put down the spear, I only told the other tribe where your hidden grain stores were.
I will give you the secret passwords to the city gates if you just let me live.
The gold they promised me was far more than you ever paid me for my loyalty.
You cannot kill me now because I am the only one who knows where the maps are hidden.
I did not want to betray my own people, but they offered me a way out of this wasteland.
I have hidden the map to the royal vault beneath the floorboards of this workshop.
The king believes this golden scepter is his greatest treasure, but I know its secret flaw.
I will keep this stolen signet ring hidden until the enemy fleet reaches the harbor.
This ornate dagger was a gift of trust that I now intend to use against them.
My master cherishes this ancient scroll, unaware that I have already leaked its contents to the rebels.
I have hidden the last three canisters of oxygen in the ventilation shaft for myself.
The colony thinks the leak was an accident, but I cut the line to weaken them.
I told the raiding party exactly where we stored the seed vault in exchange for safety.
While the others sleep, I am diverting the remaining power cells to my own private pod.
I whispered the secret entrance code to the enemy so they could take the water well.
